---
title: "ModelSummaries.jl — Publication-Quality Regression Tables"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    include-in-header:
        text: |
            \usepackage{threeparttable}
            \usepackage{amssymb}
    keep-tex: true
  typst:
    keep-typ: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
engine: julia
---

## Setup

```{julia}
using ModelSummaries
using SummaryTables: Concat, Superscript
using GLM, DataFrames, FixedEffectModels, RDatasets, CategoricalArrays
using CovarianceMatrices
using LaTeXStrings
using Random; Random.seed!(887766)
```

## Basic usage

`modelsummary` takes one or more regression models and returns a
`SummaryTables.Table` that renders natively to HTML, LaTeX, and Typst.

```{julia}
df = RDatasets.dataset("datasets", "iris")
df.isSmall = df.SepalWidth .< 2.9

m1 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth), df)
m2 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength), df)
m3 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + PetalWidth), df)

modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true)
```

## Robust standard errors

Use `CovarianceMatrices.jl` to compute heteroskedasticity-robust standard
errors. The `+` operator pairs a fitted model with a variance estimator:

```{julia}
modelsummary(m2, m2 + vcov(HC0()), m2 + vcov(HC3());
    stars = true,
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2, AdjR2, VcovType],
)
```

## Math labels with `BackendMath`

Variable labels can include math that renders correctly across all backends.
Only the `latex` keyword is required — HTML, Typst, and plain-text
representations are derived automatically:

```{julia}
modelsummary(m2, m2 + vcov(HC3());
    stars  = true,
    labels = Dict(
        "SepalWidth"  => BackendMath(latex = raw"Sepal width ($\beta_1$)"),
        "PetalLength" => BackendMath(latex = raw"Petal length ($\beta_2$)"),
    ),
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2, AdjR2, VcovType],
)
```

For full control, override any backend explicitly:

```{julia}
#| eval: false
BackendMath(
    latex = raw"$\hat{\sigma}^2$",
    typst = raw"$hat(sigma)^2$",       # Typst math syntax differs
    html  = "&sigma;&#x0302;<sup>2</sup>",
    text  = "σ̂²",
)
```

## Groups, labels, and extra statistics

```{julia}
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3;
    groups = ["Parsimonious" "Parsimonious" "Full"],
    labels = Dict(
        "SepalWidth"  => "Sepal width",
        "PetalLength" => "Petal length",
        "PetalWidth"  => "Petal width",
    ),
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2, AdjR2, AIC],
)
```

## Fixed-effects models with checkmarks

Use `yes_indicator = Checkmark` to replace the default `"Yes"` with a
backend-adaptive checkmark (`\checkmark` in LaTeX, `✓` elsewhere):

```{julia}
fe1 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + fe(Species)))
fe2 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + fe(Species)))
fe3 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + fe(Species) + fe(isSmall)))
fe4 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength), df)

modelsummary(m1, fe1, fe2, fe3;
    stars        = true,
    yes_indicator = Checkmark,
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2],
    labels = Dict("isSmall" => "Small")
)
```

## Estimator labels in the header

When columns use different estimators, pass `estimator_names` to get a
labelled header row. Consecutive equal names merge automatically.

```{julia}
modelsummary(m1, fe1, fe2, fe3, fe4 + vcov(CR1(df.Species));
    stars           = false,
    estimator_names = ["OLS", "FE", "FE", "FE", "OLS"],
    yes_indicator    = Checkmark,
)
```

## Publication-quality table

Combine all the features — `estimator_names`, `yes_indicator`, `custom_lines`,
and `footnotes` — to replicate a complex academic table in a single call,
with no manual `Cell` construction needed.

```{julia}
#| code-fold: false

# Simulated panel data
N, T = 30, 20
panel = DataFrame(
    country = repeat(1:N, inner = T),
    year    = repeat(2001:2000+T, outer = N),
    Δd_HH   = randn(N * T),
    Δd_F    = randn(N * T),
    trend   = repeat(1:T, outer = N) ./ T,
)
panel.Δy = -0.32 .* panel.Δd_HH .- 0.06 .* panel.Δd_F .+
           0.4 .* randn(N * T)
panel.country_cat = categorical(panel.country)
panel.year_cat    = categorical(panel.year)

# Four specifications
spec1 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat)))
spec2 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat)))
spec3 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + trend + fe(country_cat)))
spec4 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat) + fe(year_cat)))

# Build the table
modelsummary(spec1, spec2, spec3, spec4;
    stars           = true,
    estimator_names = ["OLS", "OLS", "OLS", "OLS"],
    yes_indicator    = Checkmark,
    labels          = Dict(
        "Δd_HH" => BackendMath(latex = raw"$\Delta d^{HH}$",
                               typst = raw"$Delta d^{HH}$"),
        "Δd_F"  => BackendMath(latex = raw"$\Delta d^{F}$",
                               typst = raw"$Delta d^{F}$"),
    ),
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2],
    custom_lines = [
        "Sample"   => ["Nonoverl.", "Nonoverl.", "Full", "Full"],
    ],
    footnotes = [
        "Standard errors in parentheses.",
        Concat(
            Superscript("***"), " p<0.01, ",
            Superscript("**"),  " p<0.05, ",
            Superscript("*"),   " p<0.1.",
        ),
        " All regressions include country fixed effects.",
    ],
)
```

## Kitchen sink — every option in one call

The following example exercises every `modelsummary` keyword argument.
Use it as a quick-reference for the full API.

```{julia}
#| code-fold: false

modelsummary(
    # Models
    m1,                        # OLS, parsimonious
    m2,                        # OLS, more controls
    m2 + vcov(HC3()),          # same model, robust SEs
    fe2,                       # fixed-effects

    # Coefficient selection & ordering
    keep  = ["SepalWidth", "PetalLength"],   # show only these
    # drop  = ["(Intercept)"],               # (alternative: hide intercept)
    order = ["PetalLength", "SepalWidth"],    # reorder rows

    # Labels & math
    labels = Dict(
        "SepalWidth"  => BackendMath(latex = raw"Sepal width ($x_1$)",
                                     typst = raw"Sepal width ($x_1$)"),
        "PetalLength" => BackendMath(latex = raw"Petal length ($x_2$)",
                                     typst = raw"Petal length ($x_2$)"),
    ),

    # Header
    groups           = ["Parsimonious" "Full" "Full" "Full"],
    estimator_names  = ["OLS", "OLS", "OLS", "FE"],
    print_depvar     = true,
    depvar_bold      = false,
    number_regressions = true,
    header_align     = :c,

    # Below-coefficient statistic
    below_statistic = StdError,     # or TStat, ConfInt, nothing
    stat_below      = true,         # false: side-by-side layout

    # Fixed effects & controls
    print_fe_section       = true,
    print_fe_suffix        = true,
    yes_indicator          = Checkmark,
    print_control_indicator = true,

    # Bottom statistics
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2, AdjR2, AIC, BIC, VcovType],
    digits       = 3,              # decimals for coefficients / SEs
    digits_stats = 4,              # decimals for summary statistics

    # Significance stars
    stars = false,

    # Alignment
    align = :r,                    # :r, :c, or :l for data columns

    # Custom rows & footnotes
    custom_lines = [
        "Sample"   => ["Full", "Full", "Full", "Full"],
        "Baseline" => ["Yes", "No", "No", "No"],
    ],
    footnotes = [
        "Standard errors in parentheses.",
    ],

    # Section ordering (default shown)
    section_order = [
        :groups, :depvar, :estimator_names, :number_regressions, :break,
        :coef, :break, :fe, :break, :controls, :break,
        :custom_lines, :stats, :extralines,
    ],

    # Output
    # file = "table.tex",        # save to file (.tex, .html, .typ)
)
```

## Writing to a file

```{julia}
#| eval: false

# LaTeX
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.tex")

# HTML
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.html")

# Typst
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.typ")
```
